report_name: Unprotected Server Leaks Personal Data of 5 Million Hotel Guests from Chekin and Gastrodat Platforms

threat_actors.name: Unknown Threat Actor
threat_actors.description: An unidentified threat actor responsible for scraping data from the Chekin and Gastrodat hospitality platforms by using compromised hotel account credentials. The actor aggregated the data on an unprotected server.
threat_actors.goals: Data theft for subsequent malicious activities such as phishing and identity theft.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor gained access to the Chekin and Gastrodat platforms by using 527 compromised accounts belonging to hotels and other properties. The credentials, including plaintext passwords and JWT tokens, were found on the exposed server.

attack_patterns.name: Automated Collection
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor used Python scripts to continuously and automatically scrape booking data from the compromised hospitality platforms.

attack_patterns.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: Python
attack_patterns.description: Python scripts were deployed by the threat actor to perform automated data scraping from the Chekin and Gastrodat platforms.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Information Repositories
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor scraped sensitive guest and booking data from the Chekin and Gastrodat hospitality software platforms, which acted as information repositories for hotel guest data.

attack_patterns.name: Data Staged
attack_patterns.description: The scraped data, totaling 6.5GB, was aggregated and stored on an unprotected server exposed to the internet, effectively staging it for the actor's use.

tools.name: Python
tools.description: The threat actor used Python scripts to perform the continuous scraping of booking data from the compromised platforms.

identities.name: Chekin
identities.description: A Spanish check-in service provider whose platform was scraped for guest data using compromised hotel accounts.

identities.name: Gastrodat
identities.description: An Austrian hotel management provider whose platform was scraped for guest data using compromised hotel accounts.

identities.name: 5 Million Hotel Guests
identities.description: A class of approximately 5 million travelers whose personal and booking information, including PII and booking details, was exposed in the data breach.

infrastructure.name: Data Aggregation Server
infrastructure.description: An unprotected server used by the threat actor to store 6.5GB of scraped data from Chekin and Gastrodat, which was left exposed to the internet.

indicators.name: Suspicious Python Scraping Script Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Python Scraping Script Execution
id: 9b1d8c3e-7f0a-4b1e-8c9a-2d3f4e5a6b7c
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of a python script with a name potentially related to scraping, as observed in the Chekin/Gastrodat data breach.
references:
  - https://cybernews.com/security/hackers-siphon-data-from-5m-hotel-guests-feeding-it-live-onto-telegram/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-15
tags:
  - attack.t1059.006
  - attack.t1119
  - attack.execution
  - attack.collection
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\python.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'scrape'
      - '.py'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate web scraping or data collection scripts.
level: medium
indicators.description: Detects the execution of a python script with a name related to scraping, based on the TTPs from the Chekin/Gastrodat data breach where Python scripts were used for automated collection.
